title: The Arrow
body:
The air was thick,
I climbed that mountain though I was sick,
The fog was dense; it hurt my chest,
I crossed every hurdle and did my best.
There I saw a deer, a two-headed deer,
In shock, I stood, as though a seer.
No sooner had I looked than an arrow flew,
Slicing through the thick fog, striking the deer's head too.
But it didn't die, only one head did.

title: And in middle of my chaos there was you
body: “And in middle of my chaos there was you”

title: Love
body:
"Love is not to just love someone but to feel them, to
understand their true nature, to love them as they are.
But as humans we expect more; we imagine a false
image of the person we love, we make them godly!"

//we think that they own our lives to such extent that
we lose our minds, we lose our self respect//
Love is much more complex. I have seen people go crazy
when they love and then lose it. Is it a game?

Where if we get the one we love, we win? If not, we
lose? No, we lose when we think that our love depends
solely on the existence of the loved one in our lives. It is
not; it is merely a failure of loving.
People say they love but do not care. Love is a mixture
of romance, care, understanding, reliability, and trust.
Love comes with sacrifices, injustice, pain, and
humiliation.

title: Ambition
body:
What an ambitious soul I am, I dream a lot yet work
very less, as if I never care to deliver the potential to my
life to an astonishing level of gratitude.

title: Alas, your heartbeat
body:
Alas, your heartbeat looping in mind's every corner for
infinity.

title: Timeless
body:
We are in a timeless waltz in the astral realm, the
universe is a big ship and you are the helm. Our dance
radiating against all odds, we inspire, conspire the
celestial bodies to dwell with us. We have become
eternal bodies, to swim in galaxies.

title: Hum Thodi Hain!
body:
Hum sab ko pasand aajayein,
chand thodi hain?
Hum sab ko lubhalein,
jaam thodi hain?
Hum sab ko rula jayein,
pyaaz thodi hain?
Hum sab ko mil-jayein,
niyaaz thodi hain?
Hum sab ko sukoon dein,
namaz thodi hain?
Hum insaan hain, farishte thodi hain!

title: Vicktor
body:
I understand now. The message hidden within the
pattern. The reason for our failures in the commune.
It's inescapable. Humanity. Our very essence. Our
emotions... Rage. Compassion. Hate. Two sides of the
same coin. Inextricably bound.

title: Maybe in another universe
body: Maybe in another universe we are together

title: Aankhein
body:
Aankhen, jo unka deedar kare wo aankhein.
Aankhein, wo jo unhe dekh kar apni haar tasleem karein
wo aankhein.
Aankhein, jise dekh kar nasha aaye,
Aankhein, jise dekhne par jannat nazar aaye.
Jab dekha uski aankhon ko to khud ko dekha, dekha to
Khuda ko bhi.

Jab dekha uski jheel si aankhein, to laga ke khud ki
parchayi dekhi.
Aankhon mein dikhta hai uske saara aasman, jab
aasmaan dekha to
barish ki boondein barsi.

Boondon mein dekha to har ek boond uski aankhon ki
gehraayi bayaan kar rahi thi.
Inn barish ki boondon ne milke jheel ki buniyaad kardi,
us jheel mein maine khud ko paaya.
Jab khud ko dekha to main uss jheel mein ghark ho raha
tha.

Jab tak main khud ko bachata tab tak main uss jheel ki
gehraiyon mein doob chuka tha.
Phir jab hosh aaya to jaana ke main to sirf uski aankhon
ko dekh raha tha.

Jab dekho tum un aankhon ko to nazrein chura lena,
warna tum bhi meri tarah ghark ho jaoge uski jheel si
aankhon mein aur
jab tak tum khud ko bachaoge tum uski mohabbat mein
barbaad ho chuke hoge.

Nahi, ye uski ghalti nahi hai ke Khuda ne uski aankhen
itni khoobsurat banayi ke dekhne wale ke hosh hi na
rahein.

title: APM 08279+52550
body:
A big cloud of mist and droplets,
It is place where we can't reach with our rockets,
My brain is bursted white & black like my home sockets,
The cloud is in the space, lonely, like me sitting in my
closet,
Empty, you have only gases, like my empty pockets.

title: The End
body:
So, they both came to an end,
The endless loop of entanglement ceased.
The effortless journey to the realm of stars was finished;
Neither did she want him back, nor did he wish to
return.

They both were ended, ashlessly burned.
Once afraid to lose,
Now they cruise through life with nothing left to
choose.

title: Departure
body:
Tera yun chale jana na mujhe bhaye,
Reh lunga main kahin bhi bas apna de de tu saya,
Mar jaun to manogi kya, sang mere sitare hoon main
laya,
Inkaar kare sab kuch ye tera kaisa hai khel, kaisi hai
maya,
Maan ja meri jaan, main tuta hua dil hoon laya.

title: Yaad
body:
In my heart, I hold your memory dear,
Though you are no longer here.
Your love was a light that shone so bright,
Guiding me through the darkest night.
I miss you more than words can say,
But I know you're with me every day.
Your spirit lives on in all that I do,
And I feel your love shining through.
Though we may be apart,
You will always hold a special place in my heart.
Your memory will never fade away,
And I'll cherish it forever and a day.
May your soul rest in peace,
And may your love never cease.
You will always be remembered,
And your memory will be forever treasured.

title: Order and Chaos
body:
I shatter my very own self to be with you, just thinking
that the arrival of death can make things better, the
death of the chaos in our minds. But does chaos die, or
does it just fade as we gain experience? I believe in faith,
but I also believe we mend the timeline by what we
think. We are cosmos, not just skin and flesh; we tend to
possess powers that we are unaware of. Our minds and
dreams hold so much of human potential.

title: Mera Chaand
body:
Raat jo aayi hai, andhera leke to aayi hai, magar chaand
noor bhi saath laaya hai.
Aur hum dono, yun khade hain is chandni mein,
Nange paon, ghaas ko choomte hue, in waadiyon mein,
jahan tum...
Mere saath ho, chahe din ho ya raat.
Tum yun meri aaghosh mein khelo, jaise hawayein mere
baalon ko chooti hain,
Waise hi tum bhi lehrao, jaise khula aanchal ho.
Tum par ye chandni yun jachti hai, jaise ye tumhara
libaas ho,
Aur hum dono Raqs-e-Junoon mein yun khushi se
jhoomein,
Ke humari mohabbat ka koi muqaam na rahe,
Ye junoon, ye pagalpan, ye bepanaah mohabbat,
Tumse mujhe har saans mein mehsoos hoti hai.
Jab tum meri aaghosh mein aati ho,
To yun meri saanson mein samaane lagti ho.
Tum... haan, tum hi to ho mera chaand, ae baagh-e-
gulistaan!

title: Many forms of love
body:
He gave himself to love,
piece by aching piece,
crossed endless seas,
spent all he had,
only to see her in another's arms.
And as the silence consumed him whole,
he whispered softly,
"Love is destruction."

Another spoke of love's dream,
where the heart found its first rhythm,
and the chaos of the world turned to melody.
In her eyes, he saw heaven on earth,
a beauty that carried him far from despair.
He fell into its depths,
never waking, never wanting to.
"Love is a dream," he murmured.

She told the tale of a love unreturned,
of a man who adored her like no other.
He poured all he had into her future,
sent her away to chase her dreams,
even as his own heart broke.
When she finally saw the love in his eyes,
it was too late, death had claimed him.
With regret, she cried,
"Love is a tragedy."

Bound by love,
but torn by the chains of caste and pride.
Years passed, families softened,
but not in time.
When her family said yes, his said no;
when his heart was ready, hers was restrained.
Caught in the endless loop of rejection, they sighed,
“Love is drama."

title: Jalan
body:
Jale tan jale man,
Jale hum har dum,
Hum lage sabse kam.
Tumhe kisi aur ke saamne hasta dekh ke jalun main
iss kadar ke aag bhi kaampe,
Darta hai jiya tumhari kajal bhari aankhon mein kisi
aur ko dekh ke,
Jalun har dum ye soch ke ke mere se zyaada koi aur
tumhe dekhe.
Jalun zamaane walon se jo aasaani se tumko dekhein
aur hum yun tarse jhalak paane ko!

title: The fallen man
body:
I ran as fast as I could, but it was raining and my
breathing was heavy. I struggled for air; my lungs
pumped fast, gasping. My black blazer is drenched. I
feel water flowing in my pocket. Now, I stand here
watching someone take, making you (which is mine)
theirs in the name of marriage. The white flower in my
pocket is dull now. My head is just a living skull now.
My life is as hell now.

title: We are our own rot
body:
We play our cards, not knowing what's next,
We give up on ourselves to rot without giving our best,
We burden our souls with all the worldly possessions,
While we devour on the vast internet scum,
unknowingly this is our obsession.
We are soaked in sins and covered with lies,
We are drowned in losses, paying our identity as a
prize.

Our love is hollowed, we love bodies not souls,
In debt of lust we lost our goals.
Our shine has gone as humans, now we are monsters,
Cannibalizing our own kind even when our heart
shatters.

title: The lost boy
body:
If you closely look in my eye
you will see a star,
A bright, shining, but a beautiful scar.
It is a reflection of my planet home,
where once I used to freely roam.
Now I lift my head up and see the sky,
whenever I do so it makes me cry.

title: Heal
body:
You are my dream come true,
my love, life and my only truth.
Upon world I chose you,
with my God this was the deal,
and for all my pain you shall heal.
Come to me, my love,
in my heart your love is shoved

title: Imtehaan
body:
Ustaad ne kaha tumhara imtehaan hoga,
Phir zindagi ne bhi kaha imtehaan hoga,
Kaha mohabbat ne bhi ki imtehaan hoga,
Tarakki karne ke liye bhi imtehaan hoga.
Aakhir mein Khuda ne bhi kaha tumhara imtehaan
hoga.

Maine pal bhar ke liye bhi na jiye,
Aur jiye to yun ke saara waqt imtehaan bita diya.
Udaas to hum tab hue jab hum nakaamiyaab thehre,
Hum na rahe kaamiyaab uss ustaad ki aankhon mein,
Zindagi ke lamhaaton mein,
Mohabbat ki raaton mein,
Tarakki ki raahon mein,
Khuda ki nigahon mein.

title: Khafa
body:
Usne humko udaas dekh ke kaha, itne khafa kyun ho?
Khuda ka diya sab kuch to hai tumhare paas, khush
raha karo.

To maine kaha, diya sab kuch Khuda ka hi hai,
Ghamzada isiliye hoon ke main khud se kuch hasil na
kar saka.

title: Ghairon ke sheesh mahlon
body:
Ghairon ke sheesh mahlon ko dekh kar
Khud ka aashiyaan na ujaado.

title: My death is close
body:
They say happy birthday!
I say I have come closer to become the compost of my
dandelions.

title: I don't want to be seasons of your life
body:
I don't want to be seasons of your life,
I want to be sky, so I stay forever even when you die,

I want to be reason for your smile,
I want you to be inspired by me,
I want you to love me like you're a madman.

title: I am grown
body:
I stretched my legs and
they slipped out from under the blanket.
And that's when I knew:
I am grown now.
Life once meant living.
Now, it means surviving.
I used to adore it.
Now I search for ways to endure it.
The burden that once shaped me
has hollowed me out.
And I know
when the burden ends,
so will I.

I've learned
it's easy to please people once,
but nearly impossible
to please them always.
Always means forever.
And nothing lasts forever.
Not me. Not them.
So I let my childishness spill out,
I do stupid things.
Because I am grown,
and I refuse to grow cold.

title: Sanson ki mala
body:
Sanson ki mala pe simroon main pi ka naam,
Par meri har saans paak to nahi,
Aur ye hawa saaf bhi nahi.
To tera paak naam har saans pe lena mujhe raas nahi.
To chalun door iss shehar se door,
Jahan hawayein paak ho.
To lun main sanyaas aur apne pi ka naam saanson pe
simroon,
Aur yahi bolun ke
Sanson ki mala pe simroon main pi ka naam.

title: Expectations
body:
Expectations... an unfulfilled dream.
I say,
"Oh, I need no expectation to keep from thee,"
but she says,
"You, my love, can."
Doth she know that her presence is what I expect,
but yet she demands it.
She, a queen, shalt have anything from me,
so I said,

"O my lady, you shalt have my expectations."
But he, who deeply in love,
forgot, expectations are deadly; they break you inside
out,
that they are thin as thread,
fragile as glass,
dense as cloud,
but love meant to him the most.

She asked, he kept expecting.
Tyranny struck him when she never kept his
expectations.
She who, a queen, a known beauty, an angel from the
skies,
could not keep her words.
He felt broken, shattered;
his own trust hath been broken.
Thus he keeps no expectation from the world;
she is and will be the world for him.

title: passionate
body:
The word passionate,
I do not want to use it in only my resume or LinkedIn.
Passionate should be my life,
Passionate should be me,
Passionate should be my breath,
Passionate should be the word whispered under the
breath by others when they see me.
Passionate should be my soul,
So should be my heart,
So is my mind.
Passionate should be the word used to describe me.
Passionate should be my home,
So should be my clothes,
So is my shoes,
Passionate should be the word which means me.

title: The darkest star
body:
Darkness is not my ally, it is what lives within me.
I do not seek light; instead, I embrace the weight of
shadow.
For only under the burden of darkness can I transform
into what we call a star,
a bright, shining star.
A glow so radiant it guides those who follow.
And so, I crave more darkness,
for the deeper it runs,
the brighter I become.

title: Talk to my demons
body:
What an utterless mouth I have.
I wish someone could hear the words I don't whisper,
the sentences I swallow,
the screams my heart makes.
I wish someone could look deeper within me,
beyond the happy face I carry.
Why must I always seem cheerful,
just to stop people from asking what's wrong?
The things my mind thinks are nothing but destruction
and chaos,
yet to balance it,
my heart stays soft,
reckoning,
fragile.
I wonder,
are the punishments we face merely the experiences we
are forced to carry through life?
Or are they only regrets,
pulling us into the darkness of guilt?

I wish people were more open, more trustable.
But now all I see is betrayal...
Or maybe I have only met those unworthy of trust.
Sometimes, I wish I were friends with demons,
for they are made of hardships, of chaos,
of struggles no one else could bear.
Perhaps they would be the ones to understand me.

title: Ashamed
body:
What a shameful soul I am,
drowning in the weight of my own guilt.
Never in my life did I imagine
that I would end like this,
in a pit of filth,

which is nothing less than what it seems.
You know what I mean, don't you?
I am ashamed of myself
for falling into the trap of the world,
all the while knowing the road I walked.
I never wanted to end up here,
like this.

I wanted to be free,
to roam, to live,
to enjoy the fruits of my labor.
But look at me now,
poor, broken.

I have become the dust that settles,
the raindrop that falls,
the star that shatters,
the land that cracks,
the sky that bursts,
the volcano that erupts,
the ocean that dries.
I am shameful.

title: Let me be rain
body:
I wish I were the rain that falls on you,
so I could touch your skin with every drop.
I wish I were the ground beneath your steps,
so your feet would never feel pain.
I wish I were the air you breathe,
not filled with smoke or pollution,
but only myself,
so every breath you take is me within you.
I wish I were the sunlight,
so when you glow,
it is my rays that make you shine.

title: Sky is my canvas
body:
The clouds became my ink, and the sky my canvas,
where I write letters to you,
letters that whisper how beautiful you are.
Truly, I have never seen such beauty
painted across the heavens.
On my canvas of sky,
You are the moon and the stars,
appearing always, everywhere.

And when the clouds turn dark,
I write to you in the rain,
so every drop carries your name,
telling the land, the trees, the forests,
and every corner of the earth
about you,
and the love I hold for you.

title: The Tormented
body:
For children whose skin was ripped and heads were
butchered,
They screamed, with blood tears.
All their hearts were filled with fear.
They were the pretty faces who had a luxurious life,
Now all that's left is agony and thoughts of knives.
Their parents were beheaded in front of them,
While they screamed and cried,
Poor little gems.

They called the world to help,
But no one answered their screams.
They were bestowed with bullets and missiles, rockets
and bombs,
With no mercy on humans, animals, or tombs.
The never-ceasing screams were loud,
Not as humans should we be proud.
Death is of children, and we paid enemies,
Now all they have are nightmares and memories.

I am tired, sick and lethargic,
Why this world surrounding me is so toxic?
Why it has to be this way?
Why can't I just be a happy soul?

WHY CAN'T I BE LOVED THE WAY I WANT?
Does not one want me?

title: Scars
body:
The tattoos you made on your body,
carved by a sharp blade,
Those deep scars lie dry, yet never heal.
I wish my lips could kiss them whole,
draw out the pain,
and breathe you back into light.
But you are so far away,
and my hands reach only shadows.

title: Khwaab
body:
Hazaron khwaab tut gaye
Sirf ek khwaab ko paane ke liye,
Hazaron din bikhar gaye
Sirf ek raat ko simatne ke liye.
Par na khwaab poore hue,
Aur na raat mein ki hui woh guzarish.
Tum bhi tanha reh gaye,
Aur hum bhi adhure.
Jeena to kab ka bhool gaye,
Ab bas maut aati hai har raat.
Na jaane kyun maut aasaan lagti hai,
Aur zindagi barbaad.

title: Dil-e-khabristaan
body:
Haan... jaane do bhaad mein pyaar ko.
Na ishq ke zulfon ki chhaon chahiye,
Na mohabbat ki raahon mein apna safar,
Na manzil hai meri woh,
Na main uska hashar.
Na sukoon ki galiyon mein tere saath tehelna hai,
Na teri aaghosh mein meri maut.

Dafan ho gaye hain saare zakhm jo tune diye,
Abhi fasaana baaki hai.
Iss banjaare-e-dil-o-jaan mein
Na hasratein baaki hain,
Na jeene ki wajah,
Na talab lagti hai,
Na khush hone ki arzoo.

Keh diya jo usne apne labon se,
ho gayi meri mohabbat ki maut,
Masla to ye bhi hai ke na suna usne tawajjo se fasana
mera,
Masla to ye bhi hai ke usne mujhpe bhi tawajjo na di,
Aur masla to ye bhi hai ki marti hui meri mohabbat ki
aakhri saanson mein uska jaana dekha,
aur ab kaisi arzoo?

Zillat-e-mukaam pe iss had tak aa chuke hain ke mera
hona na hona usko nahi lubhata,
Na use mujhse dilkashi hai, nahi dil-e-arzoo,
Abhi to bas banjar-e-khabaristan hai ye dil ki.
Iss dil ka hona na hona usko nahi lubhata,
Seene se nikal ke ruh doob chuki hai zillat se bhare
dariya mein,

Par uska aana na aana, mere bas mein nahi.
Abhi to na ranj baaki hai, na gham, na sukoon
maujood hai,
aur na tum

title: Khairiyat na pucho
body:
Khairiyat kya puchte ho,
Jism-o-badan tandurust hain,
Par kab tak iski koi zamaanat nahi.
Rooh to ghark hue zamaana beeta,
Ab woh ghane andheron mein gumshuda hai.
Aur dimaag ko kaamyaabi se matlab hai bas,
Aur dil?
Uska mat pucho,
Ke ab uska sirf ek hi kaam hai,
Dhadakna,
Aur uss dhadkan mein bhi koi awaaz nahi.

title: El Matador
body:
A man riding the bull,
gripping tight,
not letting go,
not until the bull throws him away.
I want to hold you like that,
like the matador holds the ride.
Clingy, yes.

Holding on with everything I have.
I am afraid of losing you,
because my life is what's at stake in this ride.
My heart is a gamble,
and I have placed all of it on you.

I wish to hold you so tightly
that when you finally throw me away,
either I fall far,
or I fall broken.

In some strange way,
this pain still feels like love.
And this distance,
maybe this is what we need
to finally face each other.
I am the matador.
And you are the bull.

title: Calm Storm
body:
I am the calm storm.
I live in chaos,
yet I crave my peace.
My mind runs wild,
while my heart begs for ease.
I break my surroundings,
I am the destroyer in my own way.
Yet if you stay beside me,
I will keep you safe from the wreck I make.

I burn.
I fall.
I rise again.
A war and a shelter in the same breath.
Loud in my thoughts,
silent in my fears,
learning to survive my own depth.

Stand with me in this violent quiet,
in this battle I call home.
I may be the storm that tears the sky,
but for you, I will be the calm.

title: Caged
body:
I am a lover of stones,
she gathers clouds in open palms.
I drink from restless streams,
she waits and smiles with the rain.
I condemn the darkness,
she learns how to glow inside it.

I hide within the shadows,
she pulls me gently into daylight.
She is a dream, and I don't sleep,
yet somehow she visits me anyway.
She flies like a free bird,
while I remain caged in my own thoughts.

She is a sweet, unguarded melody,
and I am the roar of an unsettled storm.
She softens the edges of my silence.
I teach her the weight of depth.

Where I am heavy, she is light,
where I am lost, she knows the way.
And somewhere between her sky and my ground,
we almost learn how to meet.

title: All we could be
body:
You are yellow, like sunshine,
and I turn toward the sun because of you.
But when night falls,
I turn to you instead,
like a sunflower that finally rests.

We stand together, watching the sun glow,
until night scatters it away
and darkness quietly takes over.
Then it is only us.
You and me,
no sky between our gazes,
watching each other instead of the light.

Moonlight falls on us like a blessing.
when the divine stands close,
we cherish each moment
as if we are inseparable.
Even when we are fading,
even when we are slowly dying,
we still choose to keep looking at each other.

Isn't the lotus beautiful?
But would it still be a lotus
if it never grew from the dirt?
You are like a lotus,
soft pink, impossibly beautiful,
rising untouched by what surrounds you.
And I am the dirt,
unloved, unseen,
something people avoid looking at.

But when you are with me,
just like a lotus in mud,
I become bearable.
I become lovable.
We are inseparable,
like mud and lotus.
No one can pull us apart,
because without each other,
we are incomplete.

Can softness rely on harshness?
Can hurt exist alongside healing?
Can sweetness survive bitterness?
Yes.
When I saw a rose, this is what came to my mind:
A rose rises from the ground
and grows thorns around itself.
Without them, it would not be whole,
only too vulnerable,
too fragile for the world.

The thorns are not born to harm,
they are born to protect.
Likewise, I am harsh at times,
not to wound,
but to guard my rose.
I may hurt,
only to save my love,
never to harm another.
And this is how my rose and I remain,
for each other,
by each other.

title: The unforgotten lines
body:
We aligned in books of fiction,
found shelter in poems of love,
where lines collide
and commas do not mind.
In haste, yet with intention, I write
lines for you,
with a quiet kind of delight.

No, I do not speak of you all the time,
yet every word that leaves my mouth
is a metaphor
carefully hiding your name.
You live between the lines of poems,
buried in the deepest meanings,
in the longest paragraphs.

You are the space between two paragraphs,
the breath I take while speaking.
You are what allows my sentences to exist,
what gives them meaning.
You are the quiet secret of the poem,
never spoken aloud,
never expelled.

I summon you in every word,
gather you in every syllable.
I search for you in every poem I read,
in every line my eyes touch.
I place a full stop to end the poem,
but you return
as the next paragraph.

title: Dune of my own voices
body:
I am stranded in a desert,
not of sand, but of sound,
voices of my own echoing,
circling me, unbound.
They whisper, they guide me
through a realm stripped of light,
yet the heat of these thoughts
makes survival a fight.

The sandstorm surrounds me
from every known side,
each grain is a voice
that has nowhere to hide.

They crawl through my mind,
press my chest, steal my breath,
as if hands made of echoes
are rehearsing my death.
And the storm softly tells me,
with a terrible grace,
"There's nothing more beautiful
than death's quiet face."
Uncertain in timing,
but promised, precise,
a guarantee written
in silence, not ice.

title: Evenings in Mysore
body:
Evenings in Mysore know my secret.

They know that I choked on my tears.
Fear of failure has its cost in my life,
tension so high, like a throat on a knife.
I have half-written letters and
unspoken words.

My words once flew like uncaged birds.
Evenings in Mysore know my secret.

They know I run away from everyone and
sit alone.
They know I have poured my tears and fed
the trees.
They know I complained to them how people tease,
how small talks hurt and never truly please.
Evenings are not so long, just long enough to finish
my coffee,
but I spend them wisely, and that is my trophy.

Evenings in Mysore know my secret.
They know I have lost love, lost interest too.
They know that the slow breeze has become my lover,
true.

They know I love my coffee when the sun is low.
They know I like sunsets, but it's hard alone
to watch them go.
They know I might have lost myself if not for the sunset
that gives me hope to live,
that after every fall into darkness,
the dawn is always there to give.

Evenings in Mysore know my secret.
I am in a garden of worries,
where I smell lies and hear gossips and hurries.
Backstabs I feel, and fake friendships I see,
all of it growing silently around me.
Evenings in Mysore know my secret.

title: Maine tere pyaar me
body:
Maine tere pyaar me dil haar ke kya paya,
kya gavaya?

Ke maine paya tujhko, jo duniya me kisi ne na paya.
Ke tujhe paake maine khud ko paya,
yun jo tu meri zindagi me aaya.
Meri bikhri hui arzooon ko mila tera saya,
maine jo pyaar khud se tha, dheere dheere gavaya.
Bhagaya maine tujhe apni zindagi se,
par mere nain sab padh lete the dil ki bandagi se.
Samajh lete the haqeeqat me kya chahta hoon,
ke tujhe paake bhi tujhe dukh nahi dena chahta hoon.

Ke mere hisse me khushi kam, dukh ke baadal zyaade
hain,
Mere ankhon se jo ashk bahe, wahi mere iraade hain.
Mere haathon ko kaampta dekh samajh leti tu,
ke main chahta hoon tujhse door rehna,
par majboor hoon main hi kyun hoon yun.
Kya ye zaroori hai, aur agar ye zaroorat hai,
to kyun zindagi ko chahiye aisi hi aadat hai?

Kyun iss zindagi me tera na hona sukha sa ehsaas lata
hai,
Ke tere chale jaane se har lamha bejaan ho jaata hai.
Ke baat tere hone ya na hone ki nahi thi kabhi,
baat thi tere dil se mujhe chahne ki sabhi.
Baat to ye bhi hai jis tarah tune pyaar ko dil se hataya,
Wo jo pyaar me pagal thi, usne khud ko kaise
samjhaya.
Jo har baat me mera naam liya karti thi,
aaj baat karna bhi zaroori nahi samajhti thi.

Par sach ye hai ke pyaar kahin jaata nahi, bas chup ho
jaata hai,
Dil ke kisi kone me dheere se so jaata hai.
Na tu bhooli hai, na main bhool paya,
Bas waqt ne dono ko khamosh banaya.
Maine tere pyaar me dil haar ke ye hi paya,
Ke tujhe paakar bhi, maine tujhe hi gavaya.

title: A Lost Man
body:
A man who loses is never truly known,
a man who has lost stands silent, alone.
A darkened window, no light, no view,
a fading frame of what once was true.

Maybe he could win, maybe try once more,
maybe strength still waits behind that door.
But when his allies leave and walk away,
he breaks within, piece by piece, each day.

When he is told he is not worthy to stand,
when the cause he held slips out of his hand,
when what he believed dissolves into air,
is he not the same man standing there?
Does one loss rewrite who he became?
Or does it just whisper a different name?
Do wins and failures define what is true,
or just how the world chooses to view?

I believe success and failure are ends we assign,
mere outcomes drawn by a shifting line.
It is always the journey that carries the flame,
more alive than the end, untouched by fame.
In trial and error, in paths we create,
in shaping our steps instead of our fate.
Like writing poetry, quiet, unseen,
where meaning lives in the space between.

I write because I love to write,
not for applause, not for sight.
Not for an audience, not for gain,
but for the rhythm inside my brain.
The search for a word, the weight of a line,
the moment a thought begins to align.
So a man is successful when ideas take hold,
when passion burns steady, quiet but bold.
But greater success is simple and pure,
when those he loves believe, and endure,
and stand beside him, steady and sure.
